sn47.19:1.0.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling among the Sumbhas, where there was a town of the Sumbhas named Sedaka. There the Blessed One addressed the bhikkhus thus:
sn47.19:1.0.2: "That's the method there," the Blessed One said. "It's just as the apprentice Medakathalika said to the teacher. ‘I will protect myself,’ bhikkhus: thus should the establishments of mindfulness be practised. ‘I will protect others,’ bhikkhus: thus should the establishments of mindfulness be practised. Protecting oneself, bhikkhus, one protects others; protecting others, one protects oneself.
sn47.19:1.0.3: "And how is it, bhikkhus, that by protecting oneself one protects others? By the pursuit, development, and cultivationof the four establishments of mindfulness. It is in such a way that by protecting oneself one protects others.
